free_text: .CYCLINDRICAL GLASS CELL, 7.5 CM DIAMETER AND 7.5 CM
HIGH, FILLED WITH DEUTERATED LIQUID SCINTILLATOR.
USED TO MEASURE THE SCATTERED NEUTRONS
-A RING SHAPED SCINTILLATOR USED TO DETECT
A FOURTH DETECTOR, A 7.0 CM DIAMETER, 7.5 CM
HIGH LIQUID HE-3 SCINTILLATOR WAS USED TO
J.C.HOPKINS AND G.C.BREIT, NUCLEAR DATA TABLES A9
T.C.MONTGOMERY ET.AL., PHYS REV C16 PAGE 499 (1977)
.A SCATTERING SAMPLE IN WHICH ONLY 58 PER CENT OF THE
HYDROGEN WAS REPLACED BY DEUTERIUM WAS USED IN
CALIBRATION MEASUREMENTS. THE RATIO OF THE HYDROGEN
TO DEUTERIUM DIFFERENTIAL SCATTERING CROSS SECTION
WAS MEASURED OVER THE ENTIRE ENERGY RANGE.
.ENERGY DEPENDENT EFFICIENCIES OF ALL DETECTORS WERE
CALCULATED USING A MONTE-CARLO METHOD AND MEASURED
RELATIVE TO EACH OTHER USING THE INCIDENT NEUTRON
AND THE FINITE GEOMETRY OF THE NEUTRON BEAM,
CROSS SECTIONS IN SUBWORK 3, AND THE FOLLOWING
H BENTE, DIPLOMA THESIS, UNIVERSITAET BOCHUM (1979)
